@mdavidallen When people are clamoring for better moderation, I truly wonder if Jack could have said “sure, we have a plan. But we need $2/month for memberships to do it right” we would have all said “Only two dollars?“
@mdavidallen It seemed stupid from the beginning not to charge users. Arguably they should charge users to post content and it should be free to view. I would pay up to $10 a month.

Trying to make Twitter be 10,000 things for 10,000 different types of use cases is absurd. It is a protocol, not a product, and we should all be able to use our own clients with our own viewing preferences (mute terms? Block groups? Show RTs?)
